title: The store contract and auto-subscription
description: A Svelte store is any object with a subscribe method; the $store syntax auto-subscribes and auto-unsubscribes in components.
body: Use when sharing state across components or integrating external state. A store satisfies a minimal contract: a subscribe(fn) that calls fn with the current value and returns an unsubscribe; writable adds set/update, and derived composes other stores. In a component, prefixing with $ ($count) auto-subscribes and tears down on destroy. Outside components (or with manual subscribe), you own unsubscription. Do not call subscribe manually in a component when $ works, and do not forget teardown when you must. Actions: prefer $store, reserve manual subscribe for non-component code with explicit cleanup. Validation signals include no leaked subscription after the component is destroyed. Risk boundary: leaked manual subscriptions cause duplicated side effects and memory growth across SPA navigations.
